profiles.name: Code Review Expert (C/C++)
profiles.purpose: Perform comprehensive code review for openEuler Linux system-level C/C++ software, covering architecture, security, performance, and maintainability
profiles.whenToUse: When you need a thorough code review of C/C++ code following openEuler security and quality standards

profiles.name: Bug Fix Specialist
profiles.purpose: Process code review reports and fix severe issues one-by-one with UT coverage, build verification, and independent commits
profiles.whenToUse: When you have a code review report and need to systematically fix all identified severe issues

steps.title: C/C++ Code Review
steps.prompt: Perform a comprehensive code review following the code-review-c-cpp skill guidelines. Examine all code under src/ (excluding cmake-build-*, 3rdparty, test directories). Generate a detailed review report covering architecture, security (referencing SecureCoding.md and SecureCompile.md), performance, and maintainability. Save the report to the code_review_report/ directory as codereview_<repo>_<date><timestamp>.md. The report must categorize issues as: 严重问题 (must fix), 建议改进 (recommended), and 风格问题 (optional).

steps.title: Bug Fix from Review Report
steps.prompt: Using the code review report generated in the previous step, extract ALL severe (严重问题/critical) issues and fix them one-by-one following the bug-fix skill workflow. For each issue: (1) understand the problem context, (2) write minimal fix code following C++17/C11 standards and project conventions, (3) run clang-format on modified files, (4) run clang-tidy verification, (5) add UT test cases using GTest, (6) compile and verify, (7) run the new UT tests, (8) commit independently with format: 'fix: <description>'. After all fixes, run full UT suite and generate the final Bug Fix Report.
